# Modular Bioinformatics Dashboard (eg. IRIDA/Galaxy)


## Preface {.unnumbered}

### 1. Vision
To build a "Walking Skeleton" for a multi-species (or any other kind of data that can be tabularized) dashboard that is modular, scalable, and easy to maintain. 

#### Our first goal 

We aimed to employ this dashboard visualize data produced by bioinformatic pipelines hosted:  
- on "Use Galaxy" based platform
- local "IRIDA" based platform
- Or to allow user to upload results produced by galaxy pipelines and visualize them on the dashboard. 

As prototyping option we chose: 
- User upload results produced from a pipeline and associated metadata

The dashboard utilizes **Shiny (Express) for Python**, **Polars** for high-performance data handling, and **Plotnine** for publication-quality, grammar-of-graphics visualizations.

Goals for initial development are indicated by [^1] 

#### Feature expectations

We hoped that this work will not only be a deliverable of a short-term project. 

Consequently we attempted to build a professional, scalable app. 
Thus we have built a "Walking Skeleton", and adopted a **Layered Architecture**.

The **Decoupling approach** of the different aspects of the app should improve layer reusability, maintainability while allowing for further extensions. It would also allow to swap to a different UI (user interface) or the Data source (e.g., move from CSVs to a SQL database) without rewriting the core visualization logic.

## 2. Core Architectural Principles

### 2.1. Decoupled Logic (Separation of Concerns)
The application is split into distinct layers:

External world
| Term | Architectural Term | Responsibility |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
| Connection to External World | Data **Connector** (BioBlend/API) | The "Bridge." It handles authentication and fetches raw data. |
| **Ingestion Layer** | Data Ingress | The "Gatekeeper." It reads CSV/JSON/VCF and ensures the file isn't corrupted. |
| Data Wrangling | **Transformer** (Tidy Layer) | The "Cleaner." Converts raw bioinfo outputs into a Tidy Data Contract (one row per observation). Uses **Polars** for high-performance data handling, "Tidy" contract via configuration file (Produceds data_tier1). |
| **Configuration Layer** | Metadata Registry | The "Brain." A JSON/YAML file that tells the dashboard: "For this species, look at column X for serotype." |
| Orchestrator Logic | The Controller [app: server_main.py]| The "Manager." In Shiny, this is your server logic that reacts to user input and calls the right functions. |
| **Visualization Factory** | Plotting Library (Agnostic) | The "Artist." Functions that take a dataframe and return a Plotly object. They don't care if the data is Salmonella or Listeria. |
| Display & Interaction | Presentation Layer (UI) [app: ui_main.py ] | The "Face." The HTML/CSS layout and the sliders/dropdowns the user clicks. |

app.py: entry point for shiny app 


2. **The Orchestrator (Logic Layer):** Handles metadata joins and applies species-specific rules via a configuration file. (Produces data_tier2). And here should be the eventuall filters
#REVIEW -  should 1 and 2 be at the same level of layer 
#REVIEW - where should the metadata check go ? which layer
#REVIEW : where should we send the filter, selections of data ? I think it should be in the orchestrator
#REVIEW I think its the metadata translation layer also


3. **The visualization Factory layer** Allow creation of plots independement of data.
Plotting defaults are passed to the plotting function via configuration files. 

3. **Dashboard App (Display and user interaction) layer):** A "thin" Shiny UI that handles user inputs and displays rendered plots. It allows also user to do simple queries on the dataset (request sent to polars) which allow sending new plots to the dashboard for data exploration 
# REVIEW we need an adaptation with queries that can be sent to the correct layer eg. for filters and data selection, to be able to change the static plot for data exploration
#REVIEW also allows to upload a **user preferences** configuration file, that can update visualisation defaults.


Fig: General Architectural Organigram (Layered Model)


```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Layer 1: Data Ingestion (Adapters to External World)"
        A[IRIDA API / Galaxy History / User Upload / Metadata]
    end

    subgraph "Layer 2: Data Wrangling (bio_wrangling)"
        B1[Polars Loader] --> C1[data_tier1 : tidy data set] 
        C1 --> B2[Polars orchestrator] 
        M[Metadata] --> B2
        B2 --> C2[data_tier2 : User queried data set]
    end

    subgraph "Layer 3: Visualisation Factory (Ploting logic : bio_viz_core)"
        D[Config (eg.species)] --> E[Plot/Graph Factory]
        C2 --> E
    end

    subgraph "Layer 4: Dashboard app: Display (app.py)"
        F[Shiny Express UI] --> G[Reactive Outputs]
        E --> G
        #REVIEW - needs draw interaction with shiny for select and data filtering
    end

    A -- "Raw Data" --> B
    B -- "Tidy-Data" --> C2
    C2 -- "Graph/Figure Object"
    G -- "Static** (evt. interactive) HTML" --> H[User Browser / ShinyExpress]
    
```
#TODO - place the "Metadata Translation Layer. (for datatier metadata and user metadata)
#TODO - Place the "configuration loader on the graph above
#TODO - where to put the user-preference config (to eg. add other defaults for plots, eg: color/fill/shape?)
#TODO update graph when clarified layer logic 

### 2.2. Implementation: The "Vizualisation Factory" logic

When the user selects a type of data; eg species: "E. coli" and "AMR" in the Shiny UI, the server performs the following logic:

1.  **Lookup:** Find `E_coli` YAML configuration file and respectives categories with columns to keep -\> `AMR` as described in the YAML. (Can be several types of categories)
2.  **Retrieve:** Get the list columns it will keep`["bla_CTX-M", "ndm-1", "tetA"]`.
3.  **Wrangle:** Tell Polars to select those specific columns from the main dataset. Eventually tidy transformation eg. long format
4.  **Plot:** Pass those columns to a Plotnine function designed to handle "Category: AMR."

### Summary of architecture compoents

| path/name | Role component | Responsibility | Technology | 
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| libs/bio_wrangling | **Data Wrangler** | Enforce Data Contract (Tidy data) | Polars |
| ? #REVIEW -   | **Config Loader** | Map Categories (AMR) to Column Names | YAML / Python Dict |
| libs/bio_wrangling #REVIEW -  should we put at different place ? | **Metadata Manager** | Join Bio-data + User CSV | Polars |
|  | **Graph Factory** | Turn "Roles" into Plots | Plotnine |
| | **Shiny App** | Capture User Input & Show Results | Shiny Express |

#REVIEW : I think we should really split by function, the tree organisation as per now is a bit unclear - should we not all keep in libs and only keep src for the shiny app ? 

### Links to layer descriptions

- [Layer 1: Data Ingestion](./modules/ingestion.qmd) 
- [Layer 2: Data Wrangling](./modules/wrangling.qmd)
- [Layer 3: Plotting logic](./modules/visualisation_factory.qmd)
- [Layer 4: Dashboard app](./modules/dashboard_app.qmd)


### Decoupling data preparation and visualisation layers


Shiny is run on a standard server (#REVIEW -  need to be decided eg. FastAPI-based) where the "Server" handles the heavy Polars lifting, and only the results (JSON/HTML) are sent to the user's browser. 

We wont use WASM (no havy data manipulation/exploration on browser) because we need scalability. 

- "Server logic" for data preparation (Polars library)
    - getting data
    - filtering unsuable data, tidying data 
    - joining with metadata


Advantages: 
- alows reduce load on visualisation app
- better scability in case of implementation of compute heavy features (eg. increase of data volumne). 
- Polars allows lazy evaluation (allowin)


### CodeBase organisation

- [Commented tree](tree.qmd)
